title: Mixed portfolio tape: TTD risk, FLY/IBRX event optionality
summary: Bounded evidence points to a mixed portfolio day: TTD carries the clearest risk stack, with weak-outlook and macro de-rating headlines aligning with bearish options flow. FLY and IBRX show bullish operational headlines and call-heavy flow, but both carry very high implied volatility and unconfirmed ThetaData alignment. B has a bullish re-rating headline and bullish flow, but several mapped news items look noisy or conflicting. The broader options evidence shows call-heavy reads in several held names without matching fresh news, so those are watch items rather than confirmed catalysts.

general.llm_summary: Today's discovery is stock-specific rather than broad-market driven: software and AI-adjacent names show mixed analyst sentiment, while biotech/radiopharma and memory remain the cleaner bullish pockets. The strongest positive reads cluster around TLX clinical/commercial catalysts and MU memory tightness, but MU also carries customer pushback risk. The clearest risk stack is in FLUT and INTU, where direct analyst caution is reinforced by competitive or coverage-related pressure.

top_opportunities.why: Phase 3 kidney-cancer radiation study launch and headlines citing revenue near US$1 billion create a positive clinical and commercial inflection read, though one Japan-guidance headline is a caveat.
Multiple bullish memory-demand signals, including tight supply beyond 2027 and a UBS price-target raise on free cash flow, support the opportunity case despite conflicting pricing-risk commentary.
Direct bullish coverage described AppLovin as an undervalued growth story with premium margins and digital advertising upside.

top_risks.why: Wells Fargo cut its price target to $137 while prediction markets reportedly captured 27% of U.S. World Cup sports-betting volume, creating both direct and competitive pressure.
Morgan Stanley cautious coverage and a separate Susquehanna pessimistic forecast outweighed the positive TurboTax product-revamp read.
Near-term earnings risk is elevated, with lower natural gas prices and a negative Earnings ESP cited ahead of Q2 results despite neutral dividend/news items.

caveats: General analysis is independent of held positions; portfolio read-throughs are listed separately.

outcome_tracking.policy: Judge every active pick every 5 trading days, append a new outcome snapshot, and keep the original pick_score immutable.

calibration_notes: pick_score is an immutable point-in-time prediction.
Outcome snapshots are appended every 5 trading days for as long as the pick remains active.
Future calibration should compare direction-aware return against SPY-relative return.

# Mixed portfolio tape: TTD risk, FLY/IBRX event optionality

Generated: 2026-07-21T14:20:03.000373+00:00
Portfolio as of: 2026-07-21T12:24:38Z

Bounded evidence points to a mixed portfolio day: TTD carries the clearest risk stack, with weak-outlook and macro de-rating headlines aligning with bearish options flow. FLY and IBRX show bullish operational headlines and call-heavy flow, but both carry very high implied volatility and unconfirmed ThetaData alignment. B has a bullish re-rating headline and bullish flow, but several mapped news items look noisy or conflicting. The broader options evidence shows call-heavy reads in several held names without matching fresh news, so those are watch items rather than confirmed catalysts.

## Daily Analysis - My Stocks
- Opportunity FLY: pick_score=57 confidence=0.83 theta_adjusted=0.83 - Firefly Aerospace received a string of fresh NASA awards, which the article describes as a positive operational catalyst.
- Risk TTD: pick_score=52 confidence=0.84 theta_adjusted=0.84 - TTD traded lower after a headline describing a weak outlook on July 18, 2026.

Outcome tracking: every 5 trading days. Judge every active pick every 5 trading days, append a new outcome snapshot, and keep the original pick_score immutable.

## Daily Analysis - General
General analysis is macro-first: 3 macro headline(s), 1 macro event(s), 23 direct company event(s), 1 generated read-through candidate(s), 0 read-through theme observation(s), ThetaData status=ok.

### Macro Climate

## Held Position Updates
- TTD: risk - Fact: TTD had bearish headlines tied to weak outlook, oil-shock/rate-pressure tech selling, and an IBM software-budget read-through; it also had a bullish retail-data and leadership update from Seven-Eleven Japan integration. Theta: Theta/options: 2026-07-21 flow_score -3.99, put_call_ratio 1.911, spot 18.64, 30d ATM IV 0.8424; ThetaData confirmation marked unconfirmed. Note: Inference: TTD is the strongest news/options alignment on the risk side because bearish headlines match put-heavy flow, while the retail-data partnership has not overridden sentiment.
- FLY: opportunity - Fact: FLY had bullish NASA-award and Alpha rocket return-to-orbit headlines, while a SpaceX Starship abort created an indirect negative sector read-through and the stock reportedly weakened despite awards. Theta: Theta/options: 2026-07-21 flow_score 9.0, put_call_ratio 0.343, spot 19.67, 30d ATM IV 1.192; ThetaData confirmation marked unconfirmed. Note: Inference: Operational news and call-heavy flow align positively, but very high IV and contradictory sector tape make this an event-volatility watch rather than a clean signal.
- IBRX: neutral - Fact: IBRX had bullish headlines around Anktiva manufacturing expansion and global expansion, alongside commentary highlighting valuation, dilution, and cash-burn risks. - IGLD: risk - Fact: No fresh bounded news event was provided for IGLD in the packet. Theta: Theta/options: 2026-07-21 flow_score -9.0, put_call_ratio 29.647, spot 20.59; IV not provided. Note: Inference: This is the clearest options-only downside watch in the held list, but it lacks a news catalyst in the bounded evidence.

## Connecting Opportunities
- ad-tech and enterprise software budget pressure: TTD weak-outlook headlines plus IBM software-budget warning read-through held=['TTD', 'IBM', 'MSFT', 'TEAM'] confidence=0.72
- commercial space execution and contract flow: FLY NASA awards and Alpha return-to-orbit headlines versus SpaceX Starship abort sector read-through held=['FLY'] confidence=0.68
- biotech manufacturing scale versus financing risk: IBRX Anktiva manufacturing/global expansion headlines alongside dilution and cash-burn commentary held=['IBRX'] confidence=0.66
- materials and hard-asset call demand: B bullish re-rating article and COPX bullish options flow, with no fresh COPX news catalyst held=['B', 'COPX'] confidence=0.55
- speculative high-IV call clusters without news confirmation: Call-heavy Theta/options reads in QS, DRTS, VIVO, TEAM, and NFLX without matching fresh bounded news held=['QS', 'DRTS', 'VIVO', 'TEAM', 'NFLX'] confidence=0.46

## Watch Items
- TTD: next ThetaData confirmation after 2026-07-21 to see whether bearish flow persists or reverses after weak-outlook headlines.
- FLY: whether NASA award and Alpha launch headlines overcome the negative SpaceX sector read-through and very high 30d ATM IV of 1.192.
- IBRX: any formal filing or company update on Anktiva manufacturing, cash runway, or dilution risk.
- B: verify ticker mapping for Magnolia Oil & Gas and Northfield/Columbia merger headlines before treating them as Barrick-relevant.
- IGLD: investigate put-heavy options read with put_call_ratio 29.647 if new news appears.
- QS, DRTS, VIVO: monitor whether high-IV bullish flow gains a real catalyst or fades.
- General theta market: breadth scanner reported scanned_tickers 0, so portfolio-level options breadth is incomplete.

## Caveats
- No browsing was performed; this uses only the bounded discovery and local ThetaData/options packet.
- Many ThetaData alignments are explicitly marked unconfirmed, and flow_score_v2 plus ThetaData put/call fields are null.
- Some IV snapshots are stale, especially IBRX and VIVO from 2026-07-14 and DRTS from 2026-07-15.
- Several news items are headline-level or market-commentary evidence rather than company filings.
- B contains probable ticker-mapping noise, including headlines that may not relate to Barrick Mining.
